title: Explicit heat grid total changes by the boundary term

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Difference Methods for Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations, SIAM, 2007, Ch. 9.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The total of the shifted heat grid update equals the original interior total plus r times the heat boundary term.
assumptions: r is a real diffusion number
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n is a natural-number range length
informal.expected_conclusion: The heat grid total balance is controlled by heatBoundaryTerm.

declarations.name: heatInteriorTotal
declarations.statement: def heatInteriorTotal (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => u (i + 1))

declarations.name: heatStepGridTotal
declarations.statement: def heatStepGridTotal (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => heatStepGrid r u i)

declarations.name: heatBoundaryTerm
declarations.statement: def heatBoundaryTerm (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := u 0 - u 1 + (u (n + 1) - u n)

declarations.name: heatStepGridTotal_eq
declarations.statement: theorem heatStepGridTotal_eq (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : heatStepGridTotal r u n = heatInteriorTotal u n + r * heatBoundaryTerm u n

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation total-balance theorem into Lean 4: The total of the shifted heat grid update equals the original interior total plus r times the heat boundary term.
tasks.target: theorem heatStepGridTotal_eq (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : heatStepGridTotal r u n = heatInteriorTotal u n + r * heatBoundaryTerm u n

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using heatStepGrid_eq_center_plus_heatSecondDifference, Finset.sum_add_distrib, Finset.mul_sum, heatSecondDifference_sum_range, and ring-normalized arithmetic.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics theorem that telescopes summed heat second differences to the boundary term.
